reasoning:
As of the knowledge cutoff in late October 2025, the Wikipedia article "List of infectious diseases" features a "Vaccine(s)" column that is broadly neglected—it is visibly empty for almost all diseases on the list, even those with universally known and administered vaccines (e.g., Hepatitis B, Influenza, Chickenpox, COVID-19). Consequently, the cell for Gonorrhea remains empty.

In reality, there is no fully approved dedicated gonorrhea vaccine yet. GSK’s experimental vaccine (NgG) is in phase 1/2 clinical trials and will not complete development by Jan 2026. While the UK NHS began an off-label vaccination program in August 2025 using an existing Meningitis B vaccine (4CMenB) that confers partial (~40%) cross-protection against gonorrhea, this news has been public for months without prompting a Wikipedia update.

For the resolution to change to "Yes", a Wikipedia editor would need to specifically update the dormant "Vaccine(s)" column for Gonorrhea to include 4CMenB before the resolution dates. Given that even diseases with actual dedicated vaccines lack this metadata on the page, the chance of an organic edit prioritizing the Gonorrhea row is extremely minimal. Therefore, the odds of a positive resolution predominantly rely on arbitrary or systematic table cleanups, which are unlikely to occur in the short forecast horizons (1 week, 1 month, and 3 months). The estimates reflect this low probability, cautiously floating to slightly higher odds over longer horizons.
